name: datastore
summary: Typed CRUD access layer for an entity, with a public interface and a separate storage handler seam.
description: Creates a datastore with interface, handler interface, and implementation class
problem: You have a domain entity that needs persistent storage and you want consistent, typed read/write access from the rest of the app without coupling consumers to the storage implementation.
appliesWhen: You have a stable entity type (Order, Subscription, Payout) that needs persistence
You want CRUD operations decoupled from the storage backend
You expect to write tests against this entity's persistence layer
You want a consistent place for entity-level read/write logic
avoidWhen: Data is transient or session-scoped (use a cache or transient store instead)
Schema is highly dynamic and varies per record (use a key-value store or JSON column instead)
You only need to read external data, never write (use a service or adapter instead)
The entity is so simple it does not need its own data layer
examples: I need to store customer orders
Where should the user data layer go
Set up a model for tracking payouts
tradeoffs: Three new files plus a DI binding. The handler interface stays empty until paired with a storage implementation (database, in-memory, etc.). Heavier than a single class for very simple cases.
outputs: A DI binding mapping the interface to the implementation
postApply: Implement a handler that fulfills {name}DatastoreHandler (use phpnomad/database-handler for a DB-backed one)
Inject {name}Datastore wherever you need persistent access to the entity
Add domain-specific query methods to the interface as needs arise

name.aiHint: Use the singular form of the entity. PascalCase. No suffixes like 'Model', 'Entity', or 'Record'.

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register DI bindings in
initializer.aiHint: Pick the initializer that already owns related bindings for this domain. If unsure, use the project's root initializer.
